correct: She picked up the phone.
explanation: Past simple of <pick up>: <picked up>.

correct: He turned on the light.
explanation: Past simple of <turn on>: <turned on>.

correct: They are looking for their keys.
They're looking for their keys.
explanation: Present continuous of <look for>: <are looking for>.

correct: I gave up the game.
explanation: Past simple of <give up>: <gave up> (irregular verb).

correct: She is putting on her coat.
She's putting on her coat.
explanation: Present continuous of <put on>: <is putting on> (double the 't').

correct: We took off our shoes.
explanation: Past simple of <take off>: <took off> (irregular verb).

correct: He will look after the children.
He'll look after the children.
explanation: Future of <look after>: <will look after>.

correct: She woke up early.
explanation: Past simple of <wake up>: <woke up> (irregular verb).

correct: He is filling in the form.
He's filling in the form.
explanation: Present continuous of <fill in>: <is filling in>.

correct: They found out the truth.
explanation: Past simple of <find out>: <found out> (irregular verb).

source: I throw away the old newspapers.
correct: I am going to throw away the old newspapers.
I'm going to throw away the old newspapers.
explanation: Future with <going to>: <am going to throw away>.

correct: She came back home late.
explanation: Past simple of <come back>: <came back> (irregular verb).

source: They carry on with the project.
correct: They are carrying on with the project.
They're carrying on with the project.
explanation: Present continuous of <carry on>: <are carrying on>.

correct: He broke down the door.
explanation: Past simple of <break down>: <broke down> (irregular verb).

correct: She didn't turn off the TV.
She did not turn off the TV.
explanation: Negative past: <didn't> + base form of the phrasal verb.

prompt: Change to a question in past simple
correct: Did he pick up the children?
explanation: Question in past: <Did> + subject + base form of phrasal verb?

source: I run into my old friend.
correct: I ran into my old friend.
explanation: Past simple of <run into>: <ran into> (irregular verb).

correct: She is cleaning up the kitchen.
She's cleaning up the kitchen.
explanation: Present continuous of <clean up>: <is cleaning up>.

correct: They set up the tent.
explanation: <Set> is the same in present and past: <set up> → <set up>.

correct: She will give back the book.
She'll give back the book.
explanation: Future of <give back>: <will give back>.

correct: He got on the bus.
explanation: Past simple of <get on>: <got on> (irregular verb).

source: I look up the word in the dictionary.
correct: I am looking up the word in the dictionary.
I'm looking up the word in the dictionary.
explanation: Present continuous of <look up>: <am looking up>.

source: She goes out with her friends.
correct: She went out with her friends.
explanation: Past simple of <go out>: <went out> (irregular verb).

source: We sit down at the table.
correct: We sat down at the table.
explanation: Past simple of <sit down>: <sat down> (irregular verb).

correct: They will sort out the problem.
They'll sort out the problem.
explanation: Future of <sort out>: <will sort out>.
